response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant systems experience — low-latency, high-throughput services handling millions of requests per second with asynchronous I/O and caching — plus a long-running geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map, which maps well onto map-data processing. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and his C++ work dates to roughly 2015; his systems-language depth is in DLang, which is adjacent but not what was asked. Recent roles are predominantly TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, and he has no direct routing, navigation guidance, or tiling pipeline experience, so the fit is promising but unproven on the core requirements.
strengths: Low-latency distributed systems experience: async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of HTTP requests/second
Deep geospatial/GIS domain background (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G speaker)
Strong engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, authored testing frameworks
concerns: Modern C++ or Rust is the core requirement, but his C++ experience is dated (~2015); systems work since is in DLang
No direct navigation, routing, or tile-generation pipeline experience — GIS work was data collection/mapping apps
Recent roles (2020-present) are primarily TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, away from systems-level engineering

key_factors.factor: Geospatial domain experience without routing/navigation specifics
